project_context.name: The State of Play
project_context.target_audience: VCs, PE investors, franchise executives, investment bankers, media executives.
project_context.emotional_tone: Authoritative, Exclusive, Serious, Insightful, Kickass & Premium
project_context.design_archetype: Swiss & High-Contrast mixed with Old Money Tech

typography.strategy: The 'Old Money Tech' approach. Radical contrast between classic editorial authority and sterile modern data.

headings.description: Used exclusively for article headlines, major section titles, and the hero section to evoke the prestige of legacy media.

body.description: Used for all article text, UI elements, and paragraphs. Highly legible, objective, and neutral.

labels.description: Used for overlines (e.g., 'FOOTBALL', 'FINANCE'), dates, author names, and UI metadata.

rules.headlines: Tight tracking (`tracking-tight`), especially on large sizes.
rules.article_body: Optimal line length of 65 characters (`max-w-[65ch]`), `text-lg` or `text-xl` on desktop, `leading-relaxed` for reading endurance.
rules.anti_pattern: Do NOT use Inter or standard sans-serif for main headlines. Do NOT center-align article text.

rules.gradients: BANNED. Use solid, flat colors only to maintain the serious publication feel.
rules.contrast: Ensure APCA/WCAG AA compliance. Use deep slate instead of pure black. Never use pure #000000 or #FFFFFF for large areas without slight tinting.
rules.cta: Use Accent Orange exclusively for high-intent conversion points (Subscribe, Buy). Primary Blue for standard interactions.

spacing_and_rhythm.philosophy: Surgical and generous. Premium publications breathe through negative space.

grids.homepage_bento: Asymmetrical magazine layouts. 12-column grid. Hero article spans 8 cols, secondary articles span 4 cols. Heavy use of 1px borders to separate content (The 'Newspaper' look).
grids.article_page: Single column, narrow wrapper (`max-w-3xl`) perfectly centered, with wide margins.

cards.style: Cardless. Do not use floating white boxes with shadows. Instead, separate articles using solid 1px borders (`border-b`, `border-r`) to mimic print columns.
cards.shadows: NONE for content. Flat and structural.
cards.radius: Zero or minimal (`rounded-none` or `rounded-sm`). Sharp edges convey seriousness.

buttons.style: Sharp or slightly rounded (`rounded-sm`). High contrast.

inputs.style: Minimalist. Bottom border only (`border-b-2`) or very crisp rectangular inputs. No soft rounded bubbles.

reading_experience.layout: Distraction-free. Narrow width (`max-w-[65ch]`) to prevent eye fatigue.
reading_experience.typography_scale: Title: `text-4xl md:text-6xl`. Subtitle: `text-xl md:text-2xl text-slate-500`. Body: `text-lg md:text-xl leading-loose`.
reading_experience.editorial_flair: Use 'Drop Caps' for the first letter of an article. Pull quotes should be massive, bold, and use the brand orange as a left border.
reading_experience.images: Full width of the container, crisp captions underneath in Mono font.

micro_interactions.philosophy: Premium means restraint. No bouncy animations.
micro_interactions.hover_states: Images: Very slow, subtle scale (`scale-105 duration-700`). Text: Color fade (`duration-200`).
micro_interactions.navigation: Sticky, glassmorphism header (`backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/80 dark:bg-[#090E17]/80`) with a strict 1px bottom border.
micro_interactions.loading: Subtle skeleton loaders or a simple linear progress bar at the top (like Bloomberg).

media.treatment: Images should feel photojournalistic, not stocky. Apply a very subtle desaturation filter (`saturate-50 hover:saturate-100 transition-all`) to thumbnails for a sophisticated, unified look.

f1_hero.description: Formula 1 race car speeding on track

cricket_floodlights.description: Cricket stadium with stands under bright sky

tennis_match.description: Tennis player on a clay court with long shadow

business_executive.description: Man in black suit standing on wooden bridge

instructions_to_main_agent: Tech Stack: React (Create React App), pure Javascript (.js/.jsx), Tailwind CSS.
Do NOT use TypeScript (.tsx/.ts) as this is a CRA environment.
Use Shadcn UI logic where applicable, but heavily override the styles to match the 'Cardless/Print' aesthetic.
Install required fonts from Google Fonts: Playfair Display, IBM Plex Sans, IBM Plex Mono.
Every interactive element, form, article link, and button MUST have a descriptive `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid='subscribe-hero-button'`).
Never use generic default Tailwind shadows. Rely on 1px borders (`border-border`) to establish hierarchy and grid structures.
Layouts MUST be mobile responsive. Images must not exceed 80vh on mobile.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file.
Always use modern button styles, but context-aware. Here, use sharp or slightly rounded authoritative buttons.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, custom selection states, and loading animations separate good from extraordinary.
Missing image categories = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN.
Components MUST use named exports, Pages MUST use default exports.
